mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.0 percent, labor market tightness signals strong consumer demand, driving corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50 percent of instances where UNRATE remains below 6.0 percent over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with stable industrial production indicates robust construction demand without supply chain bottlenecks, leading to positive earnings revisions and equity outperformance.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or SPX returns do not significantly outperform when both proxies move in the specified direction.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising rate environment, accelerating consumer prices force monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuation multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite rising inflation and rates, invalidating the discount rate compression mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly reflects aggregate economic expansion, which translates to higher corporate revenues and positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no significant correlation with GDPC1 growth direction over a 5-year walk-forward test, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 in rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production dropping below 95.0 signals manufacturing contraction and supply-side friction, which increases input costs and reduces profit margins, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 55 percent of instances where INDPRO remains below 95.0, contradicting the supply constraint thesis.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls coupled with declining consumer credit availability indicates wage growth outpacing borrowing capacity, signaling a consumption slowdown that drags down earnings expectations.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in at least 40 percent of out-of-sample instances over a 3-year period, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recoveries, a rapid rebound in housing starts signals pent-up consumer demand and financial sector optimism, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns as cyclicals rally.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises more than 5 percent month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50 percent of triggered instances during post-shock periods, invalidating the housing-led confidence channel.
